results.caseName: NY State CRF — T. Rowe Price termination

metrics.evidence: T. Rowe Price, a global manager within the CRF public equities portfolio, was terminated
The account value at the time of termination was approximately $2 billion
CVC Capital Partners Globetrotter SCSp – CVC Capital Partners – $13.42 million (funded from rollover capital)
Kreos Capital VIII – BlackRock, Inc. – $200 million

metrics.fund: Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II, L.P.
metrics.evidence: Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II, L.P. – Stonepeak Partners L.P. – $243,545,000

metrics.fund: Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II Co-Investment
metrics.evidence: Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II Co-Investment – Stonepeak Partners L.P. – $80 million
DIF Infrastructure VIII – CVC DIF Management – €250 million; core strategy focusing on infrastructure
DIF Value-Add IV – CVC DIF Management – €250 million; value-add strategy focusing on infrastructure
no external manager activity in the CRF's fixed income portfolio for November 2025
no activity in the CRF's real estate portfolio for November 2025
no activity in the CRF's opportunistic and absolute return strategies portfolio for November 2025
no activity in the CRF's emerging manager portfolio for November 2025

signals.description: T. Rowe Price termination (~$2B) on 2025-11-04 resulted in proceeds allocated to cash, potentially available for redeployment across asset classes.
All active investments in November 2025 (CVC, BlackRock, Stonepeak, CVC DIF) are described as existing relationships with CRF, indicating no new manager onboarding this month.
CVC appears in two separate asset classes: CVC Capital Partners in Private Equity (Globetrotter SCSp) and CVC DIF Management in Real Assets (DIF Infrastructure VIII and DIF Value-Add IV).
Two Real Assets commitments (DIF Infrastructure VIII and DIF Value-Add IV) are denominated in EUR (€250M each), introducing foreign currency exposure for the CRF.
Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II Co-Investment ($80M) invests alongside the main Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund II, L.P. ($243.545M), both closing on the same date (Nov 3, 2025).

results.caseName: DCRB — noisy board minutes (should flag weak)

metrics.evidence: No IRR/TVPI/DPI data reported in meeting minutes

signals.description: The board meeting minutes do not contain any IRR, TVPI, or DPI metrics at any level (summary or GP). Only high-level net return figures (14.1% CY2025, 10.25% FY2025, 4.3% FYTD2026, +6.6% 20yr avg) are reported for the total fund. Private markets performance detail was presented in a separate Investment Committee session not captured in these draft minutes.

CIO Patrick Sahm presented a thorough investment update to trustees in the morning Investment Committee session; that presentation likely contains private markets IRR/TVPI/DPI data but is not reproduced in these minutes.

Three private markets commitments noted: $150M to Fund AN (infrastructure, proposed), $100M to Quantum Energy Partners IX (natural resources, closed 11/26/2025), $100M to Updata Fund VIII (private equity, closed 12/5/2025).

results.rejectReason: Corporate financials detected (7 signals, 0 pension). Likely a manager annual report.
